app.name: React Redux Boilerplate
app.one_liner: A production-ready, batteries-included starter template for building long-lived React 19 applications with Redux Toolkit 2, vertical slice architecture, and full CI/CD automation.

screens.name: Development Dashboard
screens.purpose: Serve as the primary entry point for local development with hot module replacement.

rust_commands.name: pnpm_build
rust_commands.purpose: Perform type-checking and produce a production build in dist/.

rust_commands.name: pnpm_check
rust_commands.purpose: Run Biome lint/format checks and TypeScript type checking.

rust_commands.name: pnpm_dev
rust_commands.purpose: Start development server with HMR at http://localhost:5173.

rust_commands.name: pnpm_e2e_local
rust_commands.purpose: Execute end-to-end tests using Cucumber and Playwright against a running dev server.

rust_commands.name: pnpm_test
rust_commands.purpose: Run unit and integration tests using Vitest.

acceptance_tests: Given a fresh clone, when I run pnpm install and pnpm dev, then the app serves at localhost:5173 without errors.
When I make a commit following Conventional Commits, then semantic-release generates correct version and changelog on push.
When I run pnpm e2e:local, then Cucumber executes Playwright E2E scenarios against the dev server.
When I run pnpm test, then all unit and integration tests pass with coverage reported.
